$IQE is critical for the AI/photonics bottleneck. Closely tied to CPO inflection. It’s going to be vital & I do see a lot of growth for it in the next few years, without doubt. We will have many winners. 

Though, personally, Soitec has the clearest monopoly (Photonics-SOI), that is why I personally prefer it over $AXTI or $IQE. Especially in terms of risk/reward. It’s also at 1-2x PS, which is why it seems like an obvious 2x very soon.

"to_index": 5854}]}, "text": "$EQR.AX Updated - Final model with 3,000% upside

I spent 100+ hours building what I think is the most detailed $EQR.AX model in existence.

820,000 words of research. 13 sheets. 129 live formulas. Every input traced to a CEO quote, SEC filing, or customs document.

The price projection based on CURRENT trends since 2025, projected scaling, all known catalysts, comments from every known Tungsten/industry professional with a 3-5 year timeline. The earnings table showing A$66M revenue transforming into A$1.5B at peak. The stock price scenario table from A$0.30 to A$4.50 base / A$12.00 Nasdaq. The probability-weighted EV of A$2.54 in the green box. And the model stats bar at the bottom.

I've used all relatable Nasdaq uplisted Tungsten companies and their respective average volume increases and applied it to $EQR.AX as an industry standard on where it would put the stock relative to its intrinsic value. This also includes other industry bottlenecks and what the market prices their premiums as such. 

It has transcipts from CEO Craig Bradshaw in various seminars, youtube videos and earnings reports. This includes sections of Production, Contracts and Commercial info, Balance sheet / Corporate speak, Exploration and expansion (such as Wolfram), CEO sourced Market Data and Competitor data (ALM, China buying African/Bolivia mines to squeeze the West more)

Lastly Third-party data from industry professionals and community members such as @Alexsei88, @UnnajaSnoop, @METhompson72, @el_miikka, @Fibonazzo, @AdventusCEO and @88_becks. 

Why the Western supply demand is broken and why Tungsten has no real ceiling based on price inelasticity.

The West has completly relied on the back of Chinese material pricing and supply for so long, that it got so complacent that it shut down its majority of domestic supply. These are things such as exploration, funding and governmental backing, which have stemmed from net-zero and eco friendly narratives. Now China are shutting the tank, the West has finally realises the situation it has created. They had no substitute for this outcome from sheer ego and lack of intuition to pursuit social issues rather than physical. 

This entire situation has left a gap which can be mathematically obvious if only you just look at a few numbers.

TOTAL WESTERN SUPPLY
2026: 17,940
2027: 22,100
2028: 26,000
2029: 31,000
2030: 34,300

TOTAL WESTERN DEMAND
2026: 24,900
2027: 31,100
2028: 34,900
2029: 37,250
2030: 40,200

Defecit: 28%  
Tungsten market is growing by a CAGR of 8%. 
Western defence being 7-8%. 
AI being 19-30%.
CAGR of production volume? 4.7% to 4.9%.
If you know anything about compounding, you can see where this is going. 

Supply
EQR (Australia + Spain)
ALM — Panasqueira (Portugal)
ALM — Sangdong Ph1 (S. Korea)
ALM — Sangdong Ph2 (S. Korea)
ALM — Gentung (Montana)
Wolfram Bergbau (Austria)
Group 6 Metals (Tasmania)
Tungsten West (UK — Hemerdon)
Guardian / BMM (USA)
Mactung (Canada — Fireweed)
Recycling (Western)
Vietnam (Masan — grey zone)

End-use and % of demand

Defence / munitions (11-14%): W = 10-20% of shell cost. NDAA mandates procurement regardless of price.  
Zero recycling. 1% military increase = 2% market increase  because munitions aren't recycled.

Oil & gas drilling (10%): W cost = 0.2-0.5% of driller profits at $300. At $3,000 = 2-5%. But oil doubled too,   offsetting. No drill hole without tungsten carbide.

Semiconductors — WF6 (3-4%): WF6 per wafer = tiny vs $20K+ wafer value. 200-layer NAND = 200 depositions. Samsung reportedly out by June 2026. No substitute for CVD tungsten in sub-22nm.

Semiconductors — wire cutting: Tungsten wire yields more chips per wafer than diamond wire. Less swarf, thinner kerf. PV industry switched because economics were better even BEFORE the price spike.

CNC machine inserts (25%): Insert = fraction of $500/hr machine rate. Nobody stops a factory over a $5 insert becoming a $50 insert. Redesign to non-W takes 3-5 years.

Nuclear fusion (new): ITER divertor = 432 tonnes of tungsten. Government-funded. No substitute — only material surviving plasma-facing conditions. ITER started series production. DEMO, STEP, SPARC follow.

PV tungsten wire (new, +198%): 4,500t demand in one year from near-zero. 20%→60% penetration. Solar deployment doesn't stop for tungsten prices, it's mandated by climate policy. 

Li-tungstate batteries (new, +300%): 12,000t demand. Solid-state battery core material. EV transition depends on it. Li₂WO₄ as electrolyte additive.  

Mining & construction (26%): Every drill hole needs tungsten carbide bits. Every excavator tooth. Every crusher liner. Mining doesn't stop because tungsten is expensive, it's the input cost of extracting everything else.   

Catalysts and timelines

2026
May-June: Samsung WF6 supply reportedly exhausted. Semiconductor tungsten crisis headlines.
June: EQR debt fully repaid. Bradshaw: 'Expect to generate significant cash.
July: Mt Carbine expansion board approval + construction begins. A$30M spend, equipment purchased.
Sep-Oct: H2 FY2026 full earnings. EBITDA massively positive. Revenue A$200-350M+ for H2.
Sep-Oct 2026: Bank coverage initiations. BofA did ALM in March, EQR is the logical next call.

2027
Nov 26-Feb 27: Mt Carbine expansion operational. Production steps from 2,500→3,500-4,000t.
Jan 1, 2027: NDAA goes live. Chinese tungsten BANNED from all US defence contracts. No workaround.
H1: AVDS/Cadence/institutional accumulation. ETF inclusion. Possible analyst day. 
Possible Nasdaq/NYSE uplisting announcement. GMTL +43% in 2 weeks on listing. ALM +200%.
H2: Peak scarcity. Defence + semis + fusion. Thompson: 'rhodium-like move'. APT $6-10K+.

2028
New supply: ALM Ph2, TUN, Fireweed/Mactung beginning. Recycling at 6-7K tonnes.
Takeover risk. Major miner or defence prime bids for EQR. When supply chain = 2 companies, buy them.

2030
Fusion fleet procurement (DEMO, STEP, SPARC). Permanent demand Pool 3.

The general consensus from all the talks I've had with industry professionals and rare metal investors gives a timeline of 3-5 years minimum to re-engineer away from W in any application. Most applications have NO substitute at all. Companies will pay anything for 3-5   years rather than redesign. (AI Capex, 1.5 Trillion U.S. Defence spending)

Once again. This entire post has been modelling on a framework of CURRENT day trends. Any fluctuation upward or downward will compound these results massively and will vary differently. 

Thank you for reading and enjoy the gains. This will most likely be my last long form Tungsten post unless anything major happens. Let's ride.

text:
Honestly fair point about the fintwit echo chamber. Most of these “next 10x” threads are just guys passing the same 5 tickers back and forth.

But SOI is a different animal. Soitec holds >95% of the photonics-SOI substrate market with 4,300 patents. Every silicon photonics chip — whether it’s going into NVIDIA’s CPO switches, Broadcom’s Tomahawk 6, or Marvell’s optical engines — starts on a Soitec wafer. There is no second source.

The stock traded at €23 six months ago because the street still models it as a declining smartphone RF company. The photonics revenue is real but buried inside a segment they won’t break out yet. New CEO from Infineon started April 1. Earnings April 30.

I get the skepticism. But lump this in with the influencer pump rotation and you’re going to miss a monopoly substrate position in the fastest-growing layer of AI infrastructure.

text:
As of April 9, 2026

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure sector is showing strong bullish momentum, led by a massive surge in $AXTI. The rally extends a multi-day run as investors position ahead of AXT's Q1 2026 earnings release on April 30, with management previously guiding for sequential revenue growth driven by accelerating indium phosphide demand tied to AI data center buildout and improving China export permit receipts (https://t.co/PMNNax9ECs).

Broad strength across the photonics value chain reflects sustained conviction in the AI infrastructure supercycle. The 800G-to-1.6T transceiver migration continues pulling demand through compound semiconductors, test equipment, and optical networking platforms, with $AEHR and $MRVL both posting strong sympathy moves alongside the substrate names.

$AXTI: +18.69%
$AEHR: +9.02%
$MRVL: +4.79%
$IQE: +4.53%
$GLW: +2.85%
$FORM: +2.78%
$GFS: +1.88%
$FPLSF: +1.65%
$KEYS: +1.45%
$FN: +1.32%
$BESIY: +1.20%
$COHR: +0.84%
$LWLG: +0.60%
$AAOI: +0.45%
$TSEM: +0.07%
$LITE: -0.21%
$SOI: -1.22%
$CIEN: -1.34%
$POET: -2.25%
$SIVEF: -2.38%
$SIVE: -3.56%
$ALMU: -6.61%
1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
AXTI, IQE, GFS, TSEM, SOI: AXTI is the clear leader here, ripping nearly 19% on pre-earnings positioning and the InP capacity doubling narrative. IQE rode the substrate sympathy wave at +4.53%. GFS and TSEM posted modest gains as foundry proxies for compound semi demand, while SOI lagged at -1.22%, likely reflecting OTC liquidity friction and limited near-term catalyst visibility.
2. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
AAOI, LITE, CIEN, COHR, FN, GLW: FN led this group at +1.32%, continuing its steady grind as the picks-and-shovels play across transceiver packaging. COHR gained modestly at +0.84% while GLW added +2.85%, both benefiting from the broader optical infrastructure bid. LITE slipped -0.21% and CIEN dropped -1.34%, suggesting some rotation out of the larger-cap optical names into higher-beta substrate plays.
3. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure
AEHR, FORM, KEYS, MRVL: AEHR surged +9.02%, riding the compound semi equipment cycle as substrate makers like AXTI signal capacity expansion. FORM added +2.78% and KEYS +1.45%, both steady performers in the test and measurement segment. MRVL climbed +4.79%, reinforcing its positioning as the custom silicon bridge between optical networking and AI compute.
4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
POET, LWLG, ALMU, SIVEF, SIVE, FPLSF, BESIY: FPLSF gained +1.65% and BESIY +1.20%, showing some life in the European photonics names. LWLG posted a modest +0.60%. The laggards here tell the familiar story: POET fell -2.25%, SIVEF -2.38%, SIVE -3.56%, and ALMU cratered -6.61%. These micro-cap photonic plays continue to suffer from dilution overhang, OTC float dynamics, and the absence of near-term revenue catalysts to match the momentum in the more established names.

The AI Photonics sector ripped across the board as a US-Iran ceasefire announcement sparked a massive risk-on rotation into beaten-down semiconductor names. Trump posted on Truth Social that he agreed to suspend attacks for two weeks after receiving a 10-point proposal from Iran. SMH jumped nearly 5%. On top of the macro relief, $AEHR gapped up 25.69% on last night's Q3 FY2026 earnings, where bookings hit $37.2M with a book-to-bill exceeding 3.5x and effective backlog reached a record $50.9M. Revenue missed at $10.3M but nobody cared. The bookings inflection validated the silicon photonics and AI burn-in thesis, sending a read-through across the entire watchlist. Every name printed green except $ALMU. NDX +2.90%, SPX +2.51%.

text:
The SOI re-rating checklist has four gates:
1. Photonics-SOI must become a named, quantified growth driver — not just a “strong momentum” footnote. Right now Soitec buries it inside “Edge & Cloud AI” and won’t break out Photonics-SOI revenue separately. They describe “another strong performance in Photonics-SOI” with “sales significantly above prior year levels”  but give no numbers. The April 30 call is Rémont’s first — if he gives Photonics-SOI a standalone revenue figure or a TAM-to-revenue bridge, that alone could catalyze a re-rating. The market can’t price what it can’t see.

2. RF-SOI inventory correction must end. This has been dragging the headline numbers for over a year. Q3’26 was still down 29% YoY as reported, with the company citing ongoing RF-SOI inventory correction . The mobile business is ~50% of revenue historically, so even as Photonics rips, the total company looks broken until RF normalizes. The street needs to hear “correction complete” or “approaching normalized run-rate” on the April 30 call.

3. Rémont needs to restore medium-term guidance. In May 2025, Soitec withdrew medium-term guidance and moved to quarterly revenue guidance, which underlines how uncertain near-term demand remains . That withdrawal crushed institutional confidence. Reinstating a multi-year framework — even a conservative one — signals the new CEO has conviction and visibility. No guidance = no institutional re-rating.

4. The market needs to connect SOI to the CPO/AI photonics narrative. On March 11, 2026, SOI surged ~15% intraday on social media commentary highlighting their near-monopoly in SOI substrates for CPO, despite no new fundamental news . That tells you the re-rating catalyst is narrative adoption, not just execution. Soitec has >95% photonics-SOI share and 4,300 Smart Cut patents — but most analysts still model it as a smartphone RF company in cyclical recovery. The moment the sell-side recategorizes SOI as “AI infrastructure” rather than “mobile semiconductor materials,” the multiple expands.
Bottom line: The stock trades at ~€50 with a consensus target around €40 — meaning analysts are still modeling the old Soitec. The re-rating isn’t one event. It’s: end the RF destocking + break out Photonics revenue + restore guidance + get reclassified by the street as AI infra. Rémont’s April 30 call is the first opportunity to check multiple boxes simultaneously.

text: TSEM (~$172) is your best risk-adjusted play. They’re the foundry making silicon photonics chips for everyone — COHR, LITE, and others all rely on them. SiPho revenue doubled from $106M to $228M in one year. Gross margins expanding from 20% to 27% in four quarters. $920M CapEx buildout underway. Profitable, $1.97 EPS, guiding 15% revenue growth. They win regardless of which transceiver company takes share. The valuation is stretched at ~87x trailing P/E but forward P/E drops to 30-35x as earnings ramp. Analyst target of $162 is actually below current price though, so the near-term upside is more of a 2027+ story as capacity comes online.

text:
If a whistleblower with journalistic and photographic credentials is consistently documenting anomalies in the sky, could it be that they wanted me to be the one to advance the alien narrative? What they didn’t anticipate, however, was my deep dive into photonics—triggered by a land dispute with a photonics expert who holds a personal vendetta. To project light, there has to be a source. So, could the jet at the top right corner of this photo be the very source of that projection? And if so, is it possible that defense contractors are pushing the alien narrative by projecting these images? After all, who stands to benefit most from this story? Follow the money.  

Does it strike you as odd that the dark anomaly in the sky is almost dead center of the photo I shot of the Seattle skyline? It stands to reason, the projection was framed for my shot. 

Tonia Allen-Gould

Silicon photonics stocks, including semi wafer suppliers for raw materials like InP, are soaring. Long term investors should be cautious here.

text:
실리콘 포토닉스 검사 수요 급증… 마텍(MA-tek)·범전(MSS), AI 광통신 시장 선점

$LITE, $AAOI, $COHR, $VIAV, $GLW

AI 데이터 센터가 고속 전송, 저전력, 고밀도 상호 연결 방향으로 발전함에 따라 실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)가 반도체 산업의 핵심 기술로 떠오르고 있습니다. 데이터 센터의 속도와 방열 요구가 높아지면서 전자의 일부를 빛으로 대체하는 이 기술은 차세대 업그레이드의 필수 요소가 되었으며, 관련 검사 및 분석 장비 수요를 강력하게 견인하고 있습니다.

[마텍(MA-tek): 첨단 공정 분석의 선두주자]
마텍은 AI 칩, 자체 개발 ASIC, 실리콘 포토닉스 기술 개발에 힘입어 재료 분석(MA)과 고장 분석(FA) 수요가 크게 늘었다고 밝혔습니다.

특히 GAA(Gate-All-Around) 구조의 미세화로 인해 TEM(투과전자현미경), SIMS(이차이온질량분석기) 등 고해상도 검사 장비의 중요성이 커졌습니다.

첨단 패키징 과정에서 발생하는 복잡한 결함을 잡아내는 분석 역량을 바탕으로, 실리콘 포토닉스를 AI 및 첨단 공정과 맞물린 핵심 성장 동력으로 삼고 있습니다.

[범전(MSS): 독자 장비와 특허로 시장 공략]
범전은 실리콘 포토닉스 검사를 '제2의 성장 곡선'으로 정하고 실리콘 포토닉스 전담 엔지니어링 부서를 설립했습니다.

자체 개발한 실리콘 포토닉스 분석 장비인 'MSS HG' 3대를 완성했으며, 핵심인 광 손실 탐지 기술은 이미 대만, 일본, 미국에서 발명 특허를 획득했습니다.

위탁 검사부터 연구개발 테스트, 그리고 고객사에 직접 분석 장비를 도입하는 서비스까지 아우르는 통합 비즈니스 모델을 구축하여 시장 내 입지를 다지고 있습니다.

[분기 실적 현황]
두 기업 모두 올해 1분기 견조한 성장세를 보였습니다.

마텍: 3월 매출 5.38억 대만 달러를 기록했으며, 1분기 전체 매출은 14.24억 대만 달러로 전년 동기 대비 15.03% 증가했습니다.

범전: 3월 매출 2.24억 대만 달러를 기록했으며, 단월 주당순이익(EPS)은 0.35 대만 달러로 전년 대비 54.81% 급증했습니다.

[범전(MSS): 독자 장비와 특허로 시장 공략] 범전은 실리콘 포토닉스 검사를 '제2의 성장 곡선'으로 정하고 실리콘 포토닉스 전담 엔지니어링 부서를 설립했습니다. 자체 개발한 실리콘 포토닉스 분석 장비인 'MSS HG' 3대를 완성했으며, 핵심인 광 손실 탐지 기술은 이미 대만, 일본, 미국에서 발명 특허를 획득했습니다. 위탁 검사부터 연구개발 테스트, 그리고 고객사에 직접 분석 장비를 도입하는 서비스까지 아우르는 통합 비즈니스 모델을 구축하여 시장 내 입지를 다지고 있습니다. [분기 실적 현황] 두 기업 모두 올해 1분기 견조한 성장세를 보였습니다. 마텍: 3월 매출 5.38억 대만 달러를 기록했으며, 1분기 전체 매출은 14.24억 대만 달러로 전년 동기 대비 15.03% 증가했습니다. 범전: 3월 매출 2.24억 대만 달러를 기록했으며, 단월 주당순이익(EPS)은 0.35 대만 달러로 전년 대비 54.81% 급증했습니다."}}}, "rest_id": "2043471945979879832", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "896", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

The photonics/optics sector (high-speed data comms, AI infrastructure) for names like $LWLG (electro-optic polymers) and $OPTX (components for comms/defense) is tied to tech capex cycles.

Failed US-Iran talks have spiked oil (Strait of Hormuz risks), adding energy cost pressure on data centers and broader risk-off flows that could trigger pullbacks in speculative growth plays.

Fed balance sheet expansion/liquidity injections remain supportive for risk assets, potentially cushioning valuations via easier money—though it risks inflating bubbles in already stretched areas.

Sector macro leans mixed short-term; watch oil and Fed signals for direction. NFA.

text: Nvidia’s adoption of Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) in its upcoming Vera Rubin AI chip platform hinges on the semiconductor testing supply chain, as the most difficult hurdle to mass production is wafer-level testing of the ‘Electronics-on-Top, Photonics-on-Bottom’ structure, which is a 3D stack of chips, like a sandwich, that uses TSMC’s SoIC advanced packaging technology, media report. The issue is testing for photonic and electrical signals differs significantly, and the industry does not have testing equipment able to do mass production volumes. If a solution cannot be found, it may hurt adoption of the technology. A number of companies are rushing to develop such equipment, including Advantest, Teradyne, FormFactor, MPI and Hermes Testing (HTSI). $NVDA $TER $FORM $6857 #semiconductors #semiconductor https://t.co/G1OGA9cBv9

text:
Silicon Photonics Mass Production Hinges on Wafer Test Chain — 'Electrical-on-Top, Optical-on-Bottom' Architecture Is Key

As AI compute demand pushes high-speed transmission efficiency to its absolute limits, 2026 is widely regarded as the pivotal year for silicon photonics (SiPh) and co-packaged optics (CPO) to enter large-scale deployment — making it an urgent new battleground the industry is racing to stake out.

Notably, the success or failure of this silicon photonics mass production campaign now rests squarely on the shoulders of the semiconductor test supply chain.

According to OSAT industry sources, silicon photonics chip testing comprises three sequential process steps, and the most formidable production barrier currently lies at the wafer-level opto-electrical integrated test — referred to as the "electrical-on-top, optical-on-bottom" (上電下光) configuration. As this test sits at the second stage of the overall test insertion flow, the industry calls it "Insertion 2."

Specifically, because TSMC employs its SoIC technology to vertically stack the electronic IC (EIC) on top of the photonic IC (PIC), the resulting Compact Universal Photonic Engine (COUPE) must pass a dual-sided wafer test — electrical probing from the top, optical probing from the bottom — in order to screen known-good die from defective ones.

The core technical bottleneck is as follows: because the test conditions for optical and electrical signals differ significantly, no production-worthy automated test solution currently exists on the market.

For this reason, Insertion 2 has been dubbed a "black hole" in silicon photonics testing within the semiconductor industry — it is neither fast enough nor accurate enough.

In other words, how to develop a low-cost, high-efficiency automated test solution is intensely challenging the R&D capabilities of ATE (automated test equipment) vendors and test interface suppliers alike. If this bottleneck cannot be overcome to achieve economies of scale, it risks significantly dampening the market's willingness to adopt CPO in the near term.

Under mounting pressure from customers' production timelines, major test equipment players Advantest and Teradyne, along with leading test interface companies FormFactor, MPI Corporation (旺矽), and Hermes Testing (漢民測試), are accelerating the development of automated test solutions to clear the wafer-level opto-electrical integration hurdle and secure a first-mover position on this beachhead.

Additionally, supply chain sources note that the remaining two test steps for silicon photonics chips — "Insertion 1," corresponding to photonic IC testing, and "Insertion 3," corresponding to post-CPO-packaging final product testing — already have relatively mature automated solutions available on the market.

Regarding Insertion 1 product positioning, Advantest stated that "Triton," an integrated solution co-developed with FormFactor, is currently undergoing equipment qualification at customer sites and is expected to be formally deployed before year-end to support PIC testing in production.

For the critical mass production bottleneck at Insertion 2, the solution proposed by Advantest and its supply chain partners is expected to complete proof of concept (PoC) in the first half of the year, with the prospect of entering a joint development phase with customers in the second half.

Industry analysis indicates that silicon photonics chips face significantly elevated requirements for stability and accuracy in high-speed transmission environments, raising the overall test technology threshold in tandem. In particular, because the cost of detecting failures in silicon photonics chips after CPO packaging is completed is extremely high, Shift-Left Testing has become critically important — phased verification at earlier stages is now seen as the last line of defense for protecting margins.

However, supply chain sources revealed that if the electrical-on-top/optical-on-bottom test bottleneck fails to achieve an equipment-level breakthrough in a timely manner, test houses may opt to selectively skip Insertion 2 during initial mass production, betting instead on Insertion 3 where solutions are well-defined — a compromise that sacrifices some yield and cost efficiency in exchange for keeping final product shipment schedules on track.

Mass Production Hinges on Wafer Test Chain — 'Electrical-on-Top, Optical-on-Bottom' Architecture Is Key As AI compute demand pushes high-speed transmission efficiency to its absolute limits, 2026 is widely regarded as the pivotal year for silicon photonics (SiPh) and co-packaged optics (CPO) to enter large-scale deployment — making it an urgent new battleground the industry is racing to stake out. Notably, the success or failure of this silicon photonics mass production campaign now rests squarely on the shoulders of the semiconductor test supply chain. According to OSAT industry sources, silicon photonics chip testing comprises three sequential process steps, and the most formidable production barrier currently lies at the wafer-level opto-electrical integrated test — referred to as the "electrical-on-top, optical-on-bottom" (上電下光) configuration. As this test sits at the second stage of the overall test insertion flow, the industry calls it "Insertion 2." Specifically, because TSMC employs its SoIC technology to vertically stack the electronic IC (EIC) on top of the photonic IC (PIC), the resulting Compact Universal Photonic Engine (COUPE) must pass a dual-sided wafer test — electrical probing from the top, optical probing from the bottom — in order to screen known-good die from defective ones. The core technical bottleneck is as follows: because the test conditions for optical and electrical signals differ significantly, no production-worthy automated test solution currently exists on the market. For this reason, Insertion 2 has been dubbed a "black hole" in silicon photonics testing within the semiconductor industry — it is neither fast enough nor accurate enough. In other words, how to develop a low-cost, high-efficiency automated test solution is intensely challenging the R&D capabilities of ATE (automated test equipment) vendors and test interface suppliers alike. If this bottleneck cannot be overcome to achieve economies of scale, it risks significantly dampening the market's willingness to adopt CPO in the near term. Under mounting pressure from customers' production timelines, major test equipment players Advantest and Teradyne, along with leading test interface companies FormFactor, MPI Corporation (旺矽), and Hermes Testing (漢民測試), are accelerating the development of automated test solutions to clear the wafer-level opto-electrical integration hurdle and secure a first-mover position on this beachhead. Additionally, supply chain sources note that the remaining two test steps for silicon photonics chips — "Insertion 1," corresponding to photonic IC testing, and "Insertion 3," corresponding to post-CPO-packaging final product testing — already have relatively mature automated solutions available on the market. Regarding Insertion 1 product positioning, Advantest stated that "Triton," an integrated solution co-developed with FormFactor, is currently undergoing equipment qualification at customer sites and is expected to be formally deployed before year-end to support PIC testing in production. For the critical mass production bottleneck at Insertion 2, the solution proposed by Advantest and its supply chain partners is expected to complete proof of concept (PoC) in the first half of the year, with the prospect of entering a joint development phase with customers in the second half. Industry analysis indicates that silicon photonics chips face significantly elevated requirements for stability and accuracy in high-speed transmission environments, raising the overall test technology threshold in tandem. In particular, because the cost of detecting failures in silicon photonics chips after CPO packaging is completed is extremely high, Shift-Left Testing has become critically important — phased verification at earlier stages is now seen as the last line of defense for protecting margins. However, supply chain sources revealed that if the electrical-on-top/optical-on-bottom test bottleneck fails to achieve an equipment-level breakthrough in a timely manner, test houses may opt to selectively skip Insertion 2 during initial mass production, betting instead on Insertion 3 where solutions are well-defined — a compromise that sacrifices some yield and cost efficiency in exchange for keeping final product shipment schedules on track."}}}, "rest_id": "2043458181746315424", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "62164", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

@LawnChairCap In terms of tech depth (aka margins & replaceability) of InP epi for SiPh & CPO, it's really not that great. Just ask Landmark.

You'll likely see a boost in revenue for $IQE in the nearterm, but margins are likely meh & it will fade quickly. Photonics-SOI and LNOI is different.

text:
The shift to Glass Substrates and Co-Packaged Optics is the biggest infrastructure pivot in a decade. If you trade AI, semicap, or photonics, this is required reading.

https://t.co/HjI1pTeIX0

$LPKF is interesting because it sits at the enabling layer of this transition. Its LIDE and glass-substrate systems are designed for defect-free through-glass vials, precise glass structuring, and production-scale advanced packaging, exactly the sort of process capability glass-core substrates need if they are going to matter in AI data centers and heterogeneous integration.

What makes this different from a normal “new tech” story is that the entire stack is moving at once. Hyperscalers want lower power, higher bandwidth, and tighter integration, and that pushes the industry toward optical I/O, co-packaged optics, and glass-based interposers that can support cleaner signal paths and better manufacturing scalability.

$LPKF is the machinery story. It is the precision laser-processing and glass-structuring pick that benefits if glass substrates become a standard platform rather than a niche experiment. The market often prices the chip designers and optics vendors first, but the equipment and process layer can be just as important once adoption starts.

Then you have the broader photonics names that represent different parts of the buildout. Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE operates in Wireless and Photonics, with photonics products tied to optical communication, sensing, and optical wireless networks, so it is exposed to the same bandwidth and light-based connectivity trend that supports CPO.

$IQE is another name that matters because it manufactures compound semiconductor materials and epitaxial wafers for photonics applications, including visible and infrared materials used in sensing and communications. That makes IQE part of the upstream materials layer that optical systems depend on, especially where compound semiconductors and III-V materials are central.

$POET is a more direct photonic-integration story because it is built around the POET Optical Interposer, a platform meant to integrate electronic and photonic devices into a single multi-chip module using wafer-level manufacturing and packaging. That positions POET squarely inside the AI/datacenter optical-connectivity narrative rather than adjacent to it.

$LITE and $COHR are the more established photonics names, and they matter because they make lasers, optical subsystems, and photonic technologies that power AI and cloud infrastructure. Nvidia’s recent strategic investment in both companies underscores how serious the industry is about securing next-generation optical supply for AI infrastructure.

text: @eyesigh558 Mobile is 52% of revenue and was down 36% year over year last quarter. That is exactly why the stock is down 75% from its highs and why the photonics segment is being ignored. The mobile downcycle is the noise

People are shifting to majors in $AMZN $GOOG $NVDA and also in photonics they are moving towards the majors... $CLS $COHR and $CREDO 

You can see it. People are rushing to secure gains in photonics but don't want to leave the sector of semi's. 

High beta options might struggle that have ran 100-200%+

$ALAB and $APH still have room.

Nothing happens to your shares. You still own the same company.

This news is bullish — it’s a rerating (market realizing $SIVE’s role in AI photonics).

Both the U.S. and international shares should rise together.
Only difference is liquidity (U.S. ticker thinner), not ownership.

Bottom line:

✅ Shares unaffected structurally
📈 Price impact = positive if thesis plays out

text:
As of April 15, 2026

The AI Photonics sector lit up today, led by $SIVEF and $SIVE ripping 35% and 30%. Sivers Semiconductors announced a collaboration with Jabil to develop a 1.6T LRO transceiver module using Sivers' DFB InP lasers for hyperscale AI datacenters at 2.5x lower power draw. (https://t.co/j4drYtj4on)

$ALMU's +16% layered on top, digesting its $4M+ U.S. government contract win from Monday. The Jabil deal confirms DFB laser sourcing as a critical bottleneck in the 800G-to-1.6T migration. Transceiver module names lagging on tariff overhang, not demand breakdown.

$SIVEF: +35.23% 
$SIVE: +30.20% 
$ALMU: +16.62% 
$IQE: +12.97% 
$SOI: +6.19% 
$SLOIF: +4.57% 
$POET: +3.87% 
$CIEN: +1.83%
$NDX: +1.40% 
$FORM: +1.04% 
$SPX: +0.80% 
$TSEM: +0.71% 
$MRVL: +0.58% 
$TER: -0.15% 
$GFS: -0.21% 
$ATEYY: -0.47% 
$LWLG: -1.06% 
$AEHR: -1.56% 
$KEYS: -1.58% 
$COHR: -1.67% 
$BESIY: -1.81% 
$AAOI: -2.62% 
$GLW: -2.63% 
$LITE: -3.37% 
$VIAV: -3.73% 
$AXTI: -6.49%

1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Photonic Laser Sources

SIVEF, SIVE, IQE, SOI, SLOIF: Today's clear winner bucket. Jabil deal is a direct read-through for InP photonics suppliers, with the market pricing in compound semiconductor wafer tightness as 1.6T module demand scales into 2H26.

AXTI, TSEM, GFS: AXTI down 6.5% on its own supply-demand timing mismatch, not thesis deterioration. TSEM and GFS caught a modest SiPho/specialty foundry bid but lack the direct InP laser catalyst.

2. Photonic Integrated Circuits & Early-Stage Materials

ALMU: Carrying Monday's $4M+ government contract momentum into today. Quantum dot laser and nonlinear photonic materials funding is direct AI datacom and quantum compute positioning. Watch the AngelTech panel April 20.

POET, LWLG, BESIY: Risk-on sympathy trade. Today's catalyst is InP-specific, so EO polymer and other platform stories get lower beta. No fundamental change.

3. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking

CIEN: Steadiest name in the group, lower tariff exposure than pure module assemblers. AAOI, LITE, COHR, GLW, VIAV all red on supply chain repricing under current trade policy. Demand thesis intact.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure

MRVL: Holding well. PAM4 DSP silicon wins regardless of which laser or module vendor takes the socket. AEHR, FORM, KEYS, TER soft across the board; no direct read-through from a laser collaboration print. KEYS' new 220 GHz lightwave analyzer is strategically relevant but not a near-term catalyst today.

The AI Photonics sector lit up today, led by $SIVEF and $SIVE ripping 35% and 30%. Sivers Semiconductors announced a collaboration with Jabil to develop a 1.6T LRO transceiver module using Sivers' DFB InP lasers for hyperscale AI datacenters at 2.5x lower power draw. (https://t.co/j4drYtj4on)

$ALMU's +16% layered on top, digesting its $4M+ U.S. government contract win from Monday. The Jabil deal confirms DFB laser sourcing as a critical bottleneck in the 800G-to-1.6T migration. Transceiver module names lagging on tariff overhang, not demand breakdown.

1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Photonic Laser Sources

SIVEF, SIVE, IQE, SOI, SLOIF: Today's clear winner bucket. Jabil deal is a direct read-through for InP photonics suppliers, with the market pricing in compound semiconductor wafer tightness as 1.6T module demand scales into 2H26.

AXTI, TSEM, GFS: AXTI down 6.5% on its own supply-demand timing mismatch, not thesis deterioration. TSEM and GFS caught a modest SiPho/specialty foundry bid but lack the direct InP laser catalyst.

2. Photonic Integrated Circuits & Early-Stage Materials

ALMU: Carrying Monday's $4M+ government contract momentum into today. Quantum dot laser and nonlinear photonic materials funding is direct AI datacom and quantum compute positioning. Watch the AngelTech panel April 20.

POET, LWLG, BESIY: Risk-on sympathy trade. Today's catalyst is InP-specific, so EO polymer and other platform stories get lower beta. No fundamental change.

3. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking

CIEN: Steadiest name in the group, lower tariff exposure than pure module assemblers. AAOI, LITE, COHR, GLW, VIAV all red on supply chain repricing under current trade policy. Demand thesis intact.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure

MRVL: Holding well. PAM4 DSP silicon wins regardless of which laser or module vendor takes the socket. The AI photonics and optical infrastructure sector is showing a sharp split today, with substrate and materials plays surging while early-stage and speculative names get hammered. $SOI led the watchlist with an +11.91% move, extending a parabolic run fueled by accelerating investor conviction in Soitec's Photonics-SOI substrate business for co-packaged optics and AI datacenter interconnects. Morningstar noted that Soitec's shares rose 50% in just five trading sessions as investors priced in a bullish outlook for the firm's silicon photonics segment. Morningstar (https://t.co/djE1rX2T9F)
The broader sector is being reshaped by a clear bifurcation: capital is rotating into names with tangible substrate and test infrastructure exposure to the AI optical buildout, while pre-revenue photonic integration plays are getting punished. $POET dropped after Wolfpack Research disclosed a short position, calling the company "an obvious stock promotion" and flagging potential PFIC tax liabilities for U.S. shareholders ahead of the April 15 filing deadline. https://t.co/RRfdqNGIy4 $SIVEF fell in sympathy as the short report reinforced broader skepticism toward pre-revenue photonic chip names. Meanwhile, SPX +2.21% and NDX +1.66% confirm this is a risk-on tape where the photonics laggards are underperforming on idiosyncratic, not macro, weakness.

1. Compound Semiconductor Substrates and Foundries
$SOI, $BESIY, $TSEM, $AXTI: The substrate tier is where capital is concentrating. SOI's parabolic move reflects the market treating Photonics-SOI wafers as a structural bottleneck for CPO adoption. BESIY (BE Semiconductor) rode the same wave on die-attach and advanced packaging exposure. TSEM continues to benefit from specialty foundry demand, while AXTI held green on InP substrate scarcity narrative despite thin volume.
$IQE, $GFS: IQE gave back -2.65% as the OTC liquidity drag persists despite the same end-market tailwinds. GFS slipped -1.28%, still digesting the auto/industrial overhang even as its RF-SOI partnership with Soitec positions it well longer term.
2. Optical Transceivers and Active Networking
$COHR, $MRVL: Coherent and Marvell both green, tracking the 800G/1.6T transceiver cycle. COHR +1.80% is steady rather than explosive here, which suggests the easy money in pluggable transceiver names may already be priced.
$LITE, $CIEN, $AAOI: The transceiver and networking names that should be benefiting from the same AI optical build are instead lagging. LITE -2.15% and CIEN -2.68% are giving back recent gains. AAOI -4.41% continues to trade heavy, potentially reflecting profit-taking after its tariff-exemption-driven run.
$GLW, $VIAV: Passive optical infrastructure names drifting lower. Not broken, just not where the heat is today.
3. Semiconductor Equipment, Test and Infrastructure
$AEHR, $FORM, $KEYS, $TER, $ATEYY: AEHR +3.84% is the standout, continuing its recent strength on wafer-level burn-in demand tied to SiC and compound semi production ramps. ATEYY (Advantest) surged +8.65%, a strong signal that test infrastructure spending for advanced packaging and photonics ICs is accelerating. FORM flat, KEYS and TER slightly red but neither signaling anything concerning.
4. Early-Stage Photonic Materials and PICs
$LWLG, $ALMU, $POET, $SIVEF: LWLG bucked the early-stage selloff with a +7.20% move, potentially on renewed interest in EO polymer modulators as CPO architectures demand novel modulator solutions beyond conventional LiNbO3. ALMU -7.36% gave back gains on thin float dynamics. POET -8.01% is the direct casualty of the Wolfpack short report, which weaponized the PFIC tax angle right before the April 15 deadline. text:
我理解的SIVE
聊完AEHR 和 AAOI 还有很大的涨幅空间
来聊聊目前上涨空间最大的一个公司SIVE
今天SIVE就+30%

SIVE

这个公司basically就是做让AI芯片传输数据的激光器
AI芯片是需要光用silicon photonics技术来传输数据的

为什么要用光来传输数据呢？
当电信号的频率极高的时候 电子不再在整根铜线内部跑 而是在铜线的表面跑 会让电阻指数级别飙升
数据中心的两张显卡交换数据的时候速度会达到1.6T
这么快的速度电信号跑不到1-2米就会衰减成没有办法识别的代码
而且为了对抗铜线的衰减，只能加大电压去推信号，产生的热量很高不仅费电还危险
最重要最重要的一点是光没有质量+没有电荷
在玻璃里传输的时候没有电阻
总结一句话来说就是
铜线的带宽是有极限的 而光的带宽是无限的

2028 2029年

660M x 10-15？

text:
GAN is the way forward … lets discuss some alpha in the Photonics Supercycle: Signals in AIXA earnings call

$AIXA had earnings call and popped up another +11%, bringing YTD upside to +130% and 1 yr upside to +288%.

You guys know that am already invested in Aixtron ($AIXA) and have written about this German champion in the past, framing it as a quintessential "hidden monopoly" within the semiconductor equipment space. I also think that this is early innings of a massive Photonics Supercycle. As AI clusters migrate from 800G to 1.6T and eventually 3.2T optical modules, the bottleneck for AI scaling has shifted from the H100/B200 compute engine to the interconnect fabric itself. You cannot run a frontier model if the data can’t move between the GPU and the memory stack fast enough. Aixtron sits at the very mouth of this funnel.

I would not regurgitate the numbers here, rather analyse and unpack the things I consider important with impact on overall AI and semisector. 

The Great Divergence: GaN vs. SiC

One of the most tactical takeaways from the call was the stark contrast between Gallium Nitride (GaN) and Silicon Carbide (SiC).

🐊 SiC Overcapacity: Management was candid about Silicon Carbide being in a state of "substantial market overcapacity," with SiC expected to be only 10% of their revenue this year. This is a warning shot for pure-play SiC substrate and device makers who are still digesting inventory.

🐊 The GaN Opportunity: Conversely, GaN is entering a golden era. Management expects AI data centers to become the largest single application for GaN power semiconductors. Because GaN is significantly more efficient than traditional silicon at high frequencies, it is becoming the standard for the power supply units (PSUs) that feed power-hungry AI servers.

For those looking to play the GaN trend beyond the equipment layer, keep an eye on these players:

• Navitas Semiconductor ($NVTS) – A US-based pure-play leader in GaN power ICs.

• Infineon Technologies ($IFX.DE / $IFNNY) – A European giant with a massive, growing footprint in GaN following their acquisition of GaN Systems.

• STMicroelectronics ($STM) – Another European heavyweight with strong GaN-on-Silicon capabilities.

• BluGlass ($BLG.AX) – An Australian-listed speculative play involved in specialized

🐊 Datacom Doubling: Management is forecasting that demand for datacom lasers will more than double YoY in 2026. This is a massive green flag for the laser makers themselves, such as Lumentum ($LITE) and Coherent ($COHR), who are racing to expand Indium Phosphide (InP) capacity. (am invested in both)

🐊The "Tool of Record" for AI Interconnects

The standout signal from the call was the performance of the G10-AsP system. Management confirmed it has become the "tool of record" for the next generation of photonic components

🐊 Order Intake Explosion: Order intake hit €171 million, a ~30% YoY increase that blew past analyst expectations. Crucially, 65% of these orders came from the optoelectronics segment

🐊Implications for the Memory (HBM) Ecosystem

While we often view memory through the lens of SK Hynix or Micron $MU, the Aixtron update provides a critical "read-through" for how these chips will be connected in the future.

🐊 Structural Bottlenecks: For those holding SK Hynix, $DRAM or Micron ($MU), the shift toward optical interconnects is the solution to the "power wall" that threatens to limit HBM performance. Aixtron’s order backlog suggests the equipment for this transition is being installed now.

🐊 Near-Stack Integration: The acceleration of Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) means that optical engines will eventually sit directly on the package alongside High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) stacks.

Look the "Photonics Supercycle" isn't a theory anymore it’s appearing in the capex cycles of the world's largest chipmakers.

And remember, none of this is financial advice. The crocodile can make errors. So do your own research.

text:
My $MRVL position is already up 35%. 

I think they are one of the safest plays into photonics. Financially very healthy and they are using their cash do to some great acquisition. 

Its Celestial AI deal adds Photonic Fabric, strengthening its position in scale-up connectivity for next-gen data centers.

They also have a strategic partnership with $NVDA. They are working together on NVLink Fusion, custom silicon, scale-up networking, and silicon photonics so customers can build AI systems that stay compatible with NVIDIA’s ecosystem.

NVIDIA announced a $2 billion equity investment in Marvell as part of the expanded partnership. 

NVIDIA gets a broader, more flexible AI hardware stack, and Marvell gets deeper access to NVIDIA’s AI factory and AI-RAN ecosystem.

I started the position at $88.66. Here is my investment thesis With AI models now having trillions of parameters, the limiting factor in compute performance is no longer the GPU itself, but the network required to synchronize the massive clusters. Marvell addresses exactly this Compute-to-Connectivity Shift challenge. The Copper Wall At 800G and 1.6T speed, traditional passive copper cables suffer from insertion loss and skin effects, limiting their effective reach to just 2–3 meters. Optical DSP Dominance To achieve the vast distances of modern AI factories, electrical signals must be converted into light. Marvell is estimated to hold a 70% market share in the 800G optical DSP market. Moreover, their 3nm 'Ara' DSP reduces power-per-bit by over 20% compared to previous generations, ensuring a leading position in the ongoing 1.6T upgrade cycle. Scale-Up Architecture Marvell is leading the charge in Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) and Photonic Fabric technologies with their $3.25 billion acquisition of Celestial AI and $540 million acquisition of XConn Technologies. This enables optical connections inside the chip package, overcoming the memory wall and reducing power consumption enormously. Financial Outperformance & The Custom Silicon Inflection Point Marvell’s high M&A activity and R&D investments are reaping significant rewards in terms of operating leverage. Record Revenue Acceleration Marvell announced a record Q4 FY2026 revenue of $2.219 billion, a year-over-year increase of 22%. The data center segment now represents 74% of total revenue. Custom Silicon Explosion Marvell’s custom silicon revenues grew from virtually zero just a few years ago to an annualized run rate of $1.5 billion. Through the co-design of its proprietary AI accelerators such as AWS Trainium and Microsoft Maia, Marvell locks in multi-generational and extremely sticky revenue streams with enormous switching costs. Explosive Cash Generation Through the use of its capital-light fabless model, Marvell has generated an enormous $1.57 billion in trailing free cash flow. For the past five years, free cash flow has compounded at an astonishing 38.4%. Valuation Realism Marvell’s Forward P/E of approximately 24.4x represents a strategic discount to its primary custom silicon industry competitor, $AVGO, which holds a Forward P/E of approximately 31x. Through the use of Reverse Discounted Cash Flow analysis, Marvell’s current $68 billion market capitalization indicates that the company’s free cash flow will compound annually at precisely 24.5% over the next 10 years. Considering the company’s guidance of nearing $11 billion in revenue for FY2027 and $15 billion for FY2028, as well as its historical free cash flow growth rate of 38.4%, the company’s free cash flow hurdle rate of 24.5% appears extremely achievable. Conclusion $MRVL represents one of the most fundamentally strong and strategically critical companies in the global AI ecosystem. Marvell’s dominance in the optical data transmission space and its rapidly expanding custom ASIC duopoly effectively insulate the company against competitive replacement. As the second wave of the AI hardware supercycle has commenced, they represent one of the top-tier infrastructure compounders. Note: this is my own financial journey, not financial advice. text:
Understanding the story is what we do best.

After $AEHR Q2 ER, we gave a $50–$100 EOY target. Then raised our near-term target to $50–$75 following the silicon photonics win.

Adding to our long-term position at $34.25 and $37.67 is now paying off.

Now the market is finally catching on!

text:
Most photonics stocks dipped amid early Iran war volatility (Feb-Mar 2026) due to oil spikes, risk-off flows, and supply fears—tech/semicon sector down ~8% overall.

Post-April 8 ceasefire, AI/optical plays like NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, and ANET rallied hardest on energy relief and trade optimism. 

More resilient: CSCO, GLW (stable networking/materials), KEYS/ONTO (test gear with defense ties). HIMX/LITE took bigger hits from chain disruptions. 

Sector poised for rebound if talks hold. Not advice—DYOR.

Both are high-risk plays in silicon photonics for AI data centers—no clear "winner" as they're pre-revenue and execution-dependent. 

$LWLG: Strong on polymer modulators, recent Tower/GF foundry deals, tier-1 customer pipeline (Stage 3), funded to 2027. Stock's run hot (new 52w highs ~$8-10).

$POET: Optical interposer/hybrid lasers closer to scale—Q2 2026 production ramp, $430M cash, LITEON partnership, OFC buzz. Recent Q4 results drove 15%+ weekly gains.

I'd lean $POET for nearer-term revenue catalysts if they hit targets; $LWLG for longer-term tech edge. DYOR, NFA.

text:
⚡️⚡️⚡️⚡️

The report from Lumentum ($LITE) CEO Michael Hurlston has sent shockwaves through the market, confirming that the bottleneck has shifted from "making the chips" to "connecting the chips." With $LITE basically booked through 2028, the industry is in a massive "Photonics Panic."
If you’re looking at that list for the most impactful plays right now, here are the top 5 tickers based on current market dynamics and recent orders:

1. Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI) 🌟🌟🌟🌟
This is the immediate "overflow" play. Just this week, they disclosed a massive $200 million+ order from a major cloud provider (likely Microsoft) for their 1.6T transceivers. As $LITE capacity vanishes, $AAOI is proving it can handle the spillover with its Texas manufacturing lines. They are no longer just a "Plan B"; they are becoming a primary lead for the next generation of AI networking.

2. Coherent ($COHR) ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
While $LITE is getting the headlines for being "sold out," Coherent is their fiercest rival and just received a $2 billion R&D investment from Nvidia. They hold the keys to EML (Electro-absorption Modulated Laser) technology, which is the gold standard for high-performance links. If $LITE can't take orders, $COHR becomes the default gatekeeper of the industry.

3. Fabrinet ($FN) 🌟🌟🌟🌟
If you want to own the "factory" for the entire sector, this is it. They physically build the modules for Nvidia, Cisco, and even Lumentum. They are currently expanding with a massive 2 million square foot facility in Thailand specifically to handle the 800G and 1.6T ramp-up. As long as the demand for light exists, $FN is the one getting paid to assemble it.

4. Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
This is a high-alpha "Quality Gatekeeper" play. When clusters cost billions, you can't afford a single laser to burn out. $AEHR just reported a record book-to-bill ratio of 3.5x, driven by demand for their FOX-XP systems that test silicon photonics at the wafer level. Their stock recently jumped over 25% following a surge in AI-related bookings, making them a major winner in the "yield matters most" phase.

5. Marvell $MRVL 🌟🌟🌟🌟
Hardware is just glass and metal without the brains. Marvell’s DSP (Digital Signal Processor) silicon is mandatory for these lasers to communicate with GPUs. While Broadcom is a giant, Marvell is often viewed as the "purer" play for AI data center infrastructure. Nvidia’s recent signals have been a huge tailwind for them, as every 1.6T module sold requires a Marvell chip inside.

The Bottom Line:
The speculative phase is over. We’ve entered the "Brutal Execution" phase. If you're betting on a "re-rating," $AAOI and $AEHR are showing the most explosive momentum right now because they are picking up the massive slack left by the $LITE capacity crunch.

24 hours ago I dropped the full Photonics supply chain map.

Most of the names I highlighted have already ripped higher - some extremely hard.

Not bad for “just optics”.

But here’s the part the market still doesn’t fully get:

Almost everything in photonics has run up… yet not all of it equally.

For many names valuations are now very stretched.

Yet not all of them are in the same place.

Some are trading at premium multiples with strong momentum.

Others are still in early stages with improving fundamentals.

And a few remain almost completely under the radar - despite sitting on massive asymmetric upside for the next leg of the AI supercycle.

So tell me honestly:

Which photonics play do YOU believe still has the biggest upside left right now?

I read every reply.

The AI industry has officially shifted from a "Compute Crunch" to a "Photonics Panic." 

$LITE CEO Michael Hurlston just delivered a seismic warning: if current order rates for US hyperscalers persist for just two more quarters, their production capacity will be completely sold out through the end of 2028.

This isn't just a corporate milestone; it’s a structural ceiling for AI growth. 

When the market leader closes its books for the next three years, the "Optical Nervous System" of AI becomes the most valuable commodity on Earth.

1⃣The Direct Capacity Alternatives (Primary Laser & Module Sources)

As hyperscalers scramble for slots that Lumentum can no longer provide, they are diverting billions to the only other firms with high-volume production lines:

➡️Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI: 

The primary beneficiary of the spillover. Their strategic partnership with Microsoft and massive Texas-based manufacturing capacity makes them the immediate "Plan B" for 800G and 1.6T modules.

➡️Coherent $COHR: 

Lumentum’s most formidable rival. They hold a massive share in EML (Electro-absorption Modulated Laser) technology. 

With LITE full, Coherent becomes the default gatekeeper for high-end optical capacity.

2⃣The Foundry & OSAT Layer (Manufacturing & Advanced Packaging)

These firms provide the physical fabrication and specialized assembly services for the industry's biggest players:

➡️Tower Semiconductor $TSEM: 

A crucial "Foundry" player. They specialize in Silicon Photonics (SiPh) fabrication, acting as the factory floor for fabless designers who need to integrate light onto silicon at scale.

➡️Fabrinet $FN: 

The "Gold Standard" of optical contract manufacturing. 

They physically assemble the complex modules for Nvidia, Cisco, and Lumentum. 

A sold-out industry means Fabrinet’s high-precision lines are the most contested real estate in tech.

3⃣The "Intelligence" Layer (DSP & Architecture Control)

Hardware is useless without the silicon that manages the signals. 

This layer dictates the efficiency of every photon:

➡️Marvell $MRVL & Broadcom $AVGO: 

The duopoly in DSP (Digital Signal Processors). Every laser module requires their silicon to "talk" to the GPU.

Marvell’s TERA platform is the mandatory brain behind the 1.6T era.

➡️MACOM Technology $MTSI: 

The pioneer of LPO (Linear Pluggable Optics). By removing the power-hungry DSP in specific short-reach links, MACOM offers a "power-saving" escape hatch for data centers hitting their electricity grid limits.

4⃣The Innovation Accelerators (Bridging the Supply Gap)

With traditional capacity blocked, these innovators are accelerating "Next-Gen" architectures to bypass the bottleneck:

➡️Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE: 

A leader in external light sources (CW-WDM) for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics). 

Sivers is essential for moving the laser from the pluggable module directly into the processor package - the holy grail of efficiency.

➡️Alumea $ALMU: 

A specialist in high-efficiency silicon photonics engines, streamlining the transition to 1.6T and 3.2T speeds where traditional optics fail.

➡️POET Technologies $POET: 

Their "Optical Interposer" is a motherboard for light, allowing for radical miniaturization and lower-cost assembly compared to traditional "active" optical alignment.

➡️Lightwave Logic $LWLG: 

Developing proprietary electro-optic polymers. These materials modulate light at speeds (200G+ per lane) that standard inorganic crystals struggle to achieve.

5⃣The Transport Giants (DCI & Global Infrastructure)

Data must move between clusters and across continents. These firms control the "Inter-City" light:

➡️Ciena $CIEN: Their WaveLogic coherent optics are the global standard for long-haul Data Center Interconnect (DCI).

➡️Nokia $NOK - Optical Networks (NOC): 

They provide the high-capacity optical transport and carrier-grade routing that form the literal backbone of the global AI internet.

6⃣The Quality Gatekeepers (Testing & Validation)

In a world of scarcity, a single "dud" laser can take down a $10B cluster. 

Yield is everything:

➡️Aehr Test Systems $AEHR: 

The kings of wafer-level "burn-in." 

Their FOX-XP systems test thousands of lasers simultaneously under extreme stress to ensure they don’t fail after installation.

➡️FormFactor $FORM: 

They provide the ultra-precise "probes" and test systems that validate optical performance on the wafer before it is even cut into chips.

7⃣The Atomic Foundation (Raw Materials & Substrates)

The "Bottleneck of Bottlenecks." 

No substrate = no laser. 

Period.

➡️AXT Inc $AXTI: 

A critical supplier of Indium Phosphide (InP) wafers—the physical medium required for the high-performance lasers that drive AI.

➡️IQE PLC $IQE: 

The masters of Epitaxy. They "grow" the complex semiconductor layers on wafers atom-by-atom. IQE is the first point of failure in the global supply chain.

➡️Soitec $SOI.PA: 

The dominant provider of SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) wafers, the essential building block for the entire Silicon Photonics movement.

⬇️Executive Summary: The Photonics Supercycle

A situation where a market leader (Lumentum) sells out production nearly 3 years in advance happens once a decade. This means the speculative phase of AI has ended, and the phase of brutal infrastructural execution has begun.

Key Investor Takeaways:

▶️Seek "Available Capacity": If LITE is full, capital and orders will immediately flow to AAOI and COHR.

▶️Watch the Foundations: Without wafers from AXTI and processes from IQE, not a single additional laser can be built. These are the true "Gatekeepers."

▶️Bet on Quality: With such massive production scales, errors are inevitable. Testing companies like AEHR and FORM will benefit from every photon produced.

▶️Innovation is Mandatory: New architectures like LPO (MACOM) or CPO (Sivers/POET) are no longer just curiosities, they are the only way to prevent AI from "suffocating" due to energy and bandwidth limits.

⬇️Question to the Community:

Analyzing the current supply chain and the fact that optical infrastructure is becoming the new "AI bottleneck", which of these companies would you invest in today at their current market valuation?

Which one has the highest potential for a "re-rating" in the coming quarters?

user_name: Phillip, These are my opinion. Audi Alteram Partem
text: Love the pride in SA science, but calling this "the fastest Internet in the world" is talking kak. Prof Forbes' group does excellent quantum photonics research, but we're still years (maybe decades) from practical long-distance entangled-photon internet. Distance, loss, and the need for cryogenically cooled detectors are massive unsolved problems. It's lab stuff, not deployed tech.

Source: @tenet_research https://t.co/w34WaIfI5T The Top 10 Ranked by 2026 Growth Momentum 1. $MU (Micron Technology) Role: High Bandwidth Memory (HBM3E/HBM4). Catalyst: Revenue up nearly 200% YoY. They are effectively sold out through 2027 as AI chips require 3x more memory than traditional servers. 2. $AVGO (Broadcom) Role: AI Networking & Custom Silicon. Catalyst: Dominates the networking "fabric" (switches) that connects 100k+ GPU clusters. AI segment growing at triple digits. 3. $VRT (Vertiv Holdings) Role: Liquid Cooling & Power Infrastructure. Catalyst: Blackwell chips run so hot they require liquid cooling. $VRT is the industry standard for keeping "AI Factories" from melting. 4. $MRVL (Marvell Technology) Role: Optical Interconnects & Custom XPUs. Catalyst: Just secured a strategic partnership from $NVDA. Optical port demand is expected to double as data transfer speed becomes the new priority. 5. $AMKR (Amkor Technology) Role: Advanced Packaging (CoWoS). Catalyst: The #1 physical bottleneck. $AMKR is tripling Capex to package memory onto $NVDA GPUs to meet massive backlogs. 6. $VICR (Vicor Corp) Role: Power Conversion Modules. Catalyst: Providing high-density power modules for AI racks. Revenue is surging as power density becomes a critical data center constraint. 7. $TSM (Taiwan Semi) Role: The Sole Foundry. Catalyst: The world's foundry. They are the only ones capable of printing the ultra-advanced 3nm and 2nm designs for $NVDA. 8. $ASML (ASML Holding) Role: Lithography Machines. Catalyst: Record order backlogs from memory makers. You literally cannot build a modern AI chip without an $ASML machine. 9. $SKHYNIX (SK Hynix) Role: HBM Memory Leader. Catalyst: Operating profits are soaring as the primary first-mover for $NVDA’s newest memory architectures. 10. $ADI (Analog Devices) Role: Power Signal Management. Catalyst: Supplies precision components that keep power stable. text:
CRDO가 DustPhotonics(이름이 왜 이래😊)를 인수한 내용을 Astera Labs가 aiXscale Photonics 인수한 내용과 버무려 글을 써봤습니다.

DustPhotonics의 광학 역량을 직접 확인하고 싶어서 역량껏 찾아봤는데, 학회 논문도 없고 coupling loss 수치도 미공개라 외부 검증에는 실패했습니다. 좀 아쉽지만 그 자체가 하나의 데이터 포인트라고 생각해서 솔직하게 다뤄봤습니다.

그리고 제가 생각한 진짜 수혜 레이어는 칩을 설계한 회사도, 칩을 산 회사도 아닙니다. 그 칩을 찍는 Foundry예요. 이걸 통해서 풀어봤습니다. paywall 뒤 내용인데 이 정도는 공개합니다😅

다들 CRDO 주가, 인수 사실에만 집중하던데, 공급망 구조로 읽으면 완전히 다른 회사가 보일 것입니다.

What this article covers: DustPhotonics' core L3C technology. Known specs exist, but coupling loss, alignment tolerance, and other key performance data have never been disclosed. No peer-reviewed device papers at any major conference. Not saying the tech is bad. Saying independent verification isn't possible. $ALAB acquired aiXscale Photonics in October 2025. Credo bought the PIC chip. Astera bought the fiber-chip coupling interface. Different value chain positions, same direction. Electrical connectivity companies are buying their way into optics. There's a missing variable here. DustPhotonics is fabless. They've been fabricating wafers at one specific foundry for five years. SiPh PDK switching costs are far higher than CMOS. Where does this optical internalization trend show up in the numbers first? text: Being up 50% doesn’t mean fully rerated. TSEM for example still trades at a significant discount to Western peers despite being a key player in the silicon photonics supply chain, the market hasn’t fully priced its positioning yet. Even large caps like Marvell aren’t fully rerated, the market is still pricing it as a networking chip company, not as a custom AI silicon platform. That re-rating alone is worth multiple turns on the multiple. Same logic applies to smaller names like POET, where the technology is real but institutional coverage is near zero. We’re still early on most of these

text:
Big day for Soitec! 

Up a solid 40% since we profiled it. 

A lot of the recent move is due to retail climbing into the hype around Photonics following @aleabitoreddit and @citrini being bullish on the stock.

Of all the photonics names, this probably remains my favorite reward to risk. 

Given the moat. The bombed out valuation from other segments (which will inflect soon), and the partnerships the company has with players like Shin Etsu.

Excited to hold this for longer and watch the thesis play out. 

I’m used to waiting much longer for a thesis to play out- and let it be known that we haven’t fundamentally seen it played out as of yet.

Thank you to the hive mind X community we have so many experts bringing these names to light!

text:
I’m really like that man

I top ticked OXY to the day boy. My ytd rivals all those accounts that been spamming Photonics and all those AI supply chain stocks. Yet I never owned any of those AI plays. Missing out on Bloom Energy hurts my heart after pumping it earlier much in the year. I could have been talking more shit against all those AI guys if I longed it but it’s whatever 😂, being able to have a return match the AI peddlers on this platform without any of their AI exposure, feels good in it of itself. I don’t have Serenity’s return though that dude is killing it, but I legit have zero influence in being able to pump stocks. If people actually listened to me and followed my plays I would crush his return too I believe 😂. Some can be turned off by my competitiveness but this is a zero sum game. Kill or be eaten. I didn’t make the rules, but ima play the game to not just win, but to dominate. I only feel good when it’s a blow out.

text: Fiber laser with anti-collision system made in Poland. Kimla builds their fiber lasers using US/German supplier IPG Photonics for the source. Most of the machine is from components made in Poland. It's actually clean, it doesn't fund the Chinese.
embedded_text:
Building a fast fiber laser is easy
protecting it from collisions at  speed is the actual problem

Kimla Anti collision system reacts 20X faster than the competition 

linear motors + height sensors that doesnt lag speed 
without safety is just expensive downtime simply waiting https://t.co/e6MNiVB5wO

text:
نجح باحثون في كشف تقني يمهد الطريق لجيل جديد من الإنترنت اللاسلكي، في تطوير نظام اتصال بصري لاسلكي يعتمد على الضوء بدلاً من موجات الراديو التقليدية، محققاً سرعات قياسية وكفاءة استثنائية في استهلاك الطاقة. الدراسة التي نشرتها مجلة “Advanced Photonics Nexus” ونقلها موقع SPIE، قدمت حلاً جذرياً لمشكلة ازدحام الشبكات وتداخل الترددات في البيئات المكتظة مثل المكاتب والمستشفيات، من خلال تحويل الإضاءة الداخلية إلى منصات لبث البيانات فائقة السرعة.
​​⁣⁣#جريدة_الفجر_الاماراتية

text:
🚀 $NVDA isn’t just selling GPUs anymore – it’s buying the AI future.

The NVIDIA AI Strategy now includes at least $18B in strategic bets on Marvell, photonics players and AI capacity deals, all to make sure that even when Amazon and Google roll custom chips, they’re still running on NVIDIA’s rails. Data center revenue is near $62B a year, GPUs own most of the AI market, and yet the company is acting like a challenger, not a champion.

Price: $183.32 (+0.68%)

Is this the smartest moat-building we’ve seen in tech, or a late-cycle overreach before AI margins compress?

#Nvidia #AI #Semiconductors

text: So far the gap has held very well. I have put on some exposure, got stopped on a couple of stocks but on balance the buys have been profitable. Currently holding $APLD, $LPTH, $TTMI, $ICHR, $LWLG. Focusing on the key themes at play here which are photonics, optics, and memory.
embedded_text:
Market gapping up substantially after hours on positive news.

I assume all of these will gap up too. Best to wait and see if they pull back intraday. Regardless, we will see more stocks setting up in the coming weeks if this is a true FTD.

text: $LWLG might be the next photonics stock to go on a crazy run. https://t.co/0bQNMzDq5U

$LWLG just signed a deal with Tower Semiconductor and the market has no idea what just happened.

Just went through an organized a list 

**Silicon Photonics (SiPh) & CPO Supply Chain**

**(Core Data Center & AI Interconnect Bottleneck)**

**Light Source (CW DFB Lasers)**
SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors)
AAOI (Applied Optoelectronics)
5802 (Sumitomo Electric)

**SiPh Foundry (SOI-based)**
TSEM (Tower Semiconductor)

**Compound Semi Foundry (III-V)**
3105 (Win Semi)

**Optical Interposers**
POET (POET Technologies)

**Substrates (SOI Wafers)**
SOI (Soitec)

**Epitaxy / Photonic Materials**
IQE (IQE plc)
2455 (Visual Photonics / VPEC)
ALMU (Aeluma)

**Epitaxy Equipment (MBE Tools)**
Riber (ALRIB)

**Advanced Packaging & OSAT (CPO Enablers)**
6451 (Shunsin Technology)
ASX (ASE Technology)

**Optical Subassembly & Packaging**
4977 (PCL Technologies)
3450 (Elite Advanced Laser)

**Fiber Arrays & Passive Interconnects**
3363 (FOCI Fiber Optic)

**Optical Modules**
COHR (Coherent)
LITE (Lumentum)

**Optical Semiconductor ICs (TIAs/Drivers)**
MTSI (MACOM)
SMTC (Semtech)

**Optical DSP & Interconnect**
CRDO (Credo Technology)
MRVL (Marvell Technology)

**Electrical Interconnect / Fabric**
ALAB (Astera Labs)

**Metrology / Inspection (Yield Control)**
268A (Rigaku Holdings Corp.)
3587 (MA-tek)
3289 (iST – Integrated Service Technology Inc.)

**Optical Test & Measurement**
6777 (Santec)

**Semiconductor Burn-in & Reliability Test**
AEHR (Aehr Test Systems)

**Speculative Modulator Materials**
LWLG (Lightwave Logic)

### **Other AI Infrastructure Bottlenecks**

**(Broader Scaling and Edge Constraints)**

**Edge AI & Enterprise Storage**
5289 (Innodisk Corp.)

**High-Power / Industrial Photonic Systems**
LASR (nLIGHT, Inc.)
6965 (Hamamatsu Photonics)

text:
$IQE $IQEPF 

Overview and bull bear debate 

Fundamental Overview

IQE is a compound semiconductor epitaxial wafer manufacturer ￼ headquartered in Cardiff, Wales. It operates through three segments: Wireless (RF devices for mobile/comms), Photonics (VCSELs, InP for sensing and datacom), and CMOS++ (advanced silicon-adjacent materials). ￼ The company serves chipmakers and OEMs from facilities in the UK, US, and Taiwan, and holds a substantial IP portfolio as a scaled epitaxy specialist. 
￼
FY2024 revenue was ~£118M, up ~2.4% YoY. ￼ FY2025 revenue came in at ~£97M — the shortfall driven by a brutal H1 where wireless revenue cratered 52% on inventory digestion and handset softness ￼, though H2 trading recovered strongly with faster-than-expected US defense funding releases and growing photonics demand from AI/datacentre. ￼ Adjusted EBITDA hit at least £2M, showing early operating leverage as utilization improved. ￼

Shares closed at ~36p on April 10, 2026, up 23% on the day ￼, with a market cap around £245M on ~979M shares outstanding. ￼ Total debt sits at ~$74M against just ~$5M cash, giving an EV of ~$176M. ￼ The 52-week range has been roughly 4.7p to 29.5p ￼ — enormous volatility, typical micro-cap.

Bull Case

•AI/datacentre photonics tailwind. IQE enters 2026 with a strong Q1 order book and improving demand visibility in datacentre and AI-related photonics. ￼ InP wafers for optical interconnects and VCSELs for 3D sensing are riding secular hyperscaler capex.

•Defense revenue acceleration. US military funding releases pulled forward into H2 2025 ￼, and GaN RF radar solutions are gaining traction in satellite comms and defense — sovereign supply chain programs in the US and Europe provide a structural demand floor.

•MicroLED optionality. IQE is developing GaN-on-Si microLEDs with consumer multinational partners for next-gen display ecosystems and high-density datacom interconnects. ￼ Management has called microLEDs the biggest potential volume driver for 8-inch compound semi. ￼

•VCSEL market leadership. IQE pioneered 6-inch and shipped the world’s first commercial 200mm VCSEL epiwafer ￼, with a patented VCSEL-on-germanium roadmap to 300mm — a meaningful moat if smartphone 3D sensing and AR/VR adoption scale.

•Operating leverage. The business is highly fixed-cost. Revenue recovery toward breakeven and beyond delivers outsized EBITDA margin expansion ￼ as fab utilization normalizes.

•Takeover/strategic review upside. IQE has explored strategic alternatives including a potential corporate sale ￼, and its IP portfolio and customer relationships make it a logical consolidation target for larger compound semi players.

Bear Case

•Balance sheet fragility. ~$74M debt against ~$5M cash ￼ leaves minimal cushion. If the recovery stalls, dilutive equity raises or distressed asset sales become real risks.

•Customer concentration. IQE is widely understood to be heavily exposed to Apple’s VCSEL supply chain. Any design-out, insourcing, or supplier diversification by Apple would be devastating.

•Cyclical whipsaw. H1 2025 wireless revenue dropped 52% YoY on inventory overhang and tariff uncertainty ￼ — the business has minimal visibility beyond a quarter or two and is at the mercy of handset OEM ordering patterns.

•Profitability still unproven at scale. Even with recovery to ~£97M revenue, adjusted EBITDA was only ~£2M ￼ — the company has been structurally unprofitable through the downturn and hasn’t demonstrated sustainable free cash flow.

•AIM liquidity risk. Sub-£250M market cap, AIM-listed, thin float — the stock can gap violently on modest volume, making position sizing and exits difficult.

•Execution risk on new platforms. MicroLED and GaN power/defense programs are still pre-revenue or early-stage. Success hinges on whether IQE can achieve operational breakeven before cash reserves are strained. ￼

text:
Silicon is not gonna be the future - photonics is. So fcking bullish on that. 

Silicon photonics is what we should try and master. 
Monolithic ICs are useful only in the analog space. 
SoC design is correlated with moore law. Moore law has hit a physical limit.

text:
Bullish on UK tech is a key reason, but other than the biasedness they're an attractive investment

But for a high level picture - Global leader in compound semis + well positioned to supply epi-wafers needed. Powerful turnaround driven by demand surge for photonics + data center infra. But on the flip, any delays in AI infra rollouts (stuck chokepoints like HBM) could materially and negatively impact rev/CF given high customer concentration in tier 1 customers

text: I think everyone is thinking about tesla / spacex /Xai advanced technology fab and terafab wrong. IMO the ATF is going to be a greenfield project, quickly getting 2nm production running, but then in parallel, be introducing photonics testing as quickly as possible.  The goal is to rapidly test and iterate each of these as quickly as possible, so by 2028 or 2029 they are at full photonic computing, at this point they outfit terrafab without the need for many EUV machines. Full photonics are incredible for orbital data centers with much lower weight, power requirements, radiation hardening and emp immunity.

text:
$CRDO just went from AEC king to full-stack AI connectivity platform.

Acquired DustPhotonics — now owns both the optical DSP and the silicon photonics PIC. 

That’s vertical integration of the two core components inside every optical transceiver.

What matters:

•Optical revenue expected to exceed $500M in FY27 — a net new growth engine on top of the AEC monopoly

•FY27 total revenue now guided 75%+ YoY growth

•Margins intact (mid-60s) — owning the PIC eliminates supplier margin stack

•Dust has hyperscaler design wins and a roadmap to 3.2T, ahead of the market

•Dual CPO/NPO roadmap (laser + microLED) — CRDO wins either way
The thesis just got wider. AECs for copper. Optical DSPs for networking silicon. ZeroFlap transceivers for bulletproof reliability. Standalone PICs for the $6B silicon photonics TAM by 2030.

One company. Every connection in the AI cluster. Copper to optical. Front-end to scale-up.

Top 5 Catalysts

1.June earnings — first print with DustPhotonics revenue contribution and updated FY27 guide with optical breakdown

2.DustPhotonics close (Q2 cal ’26) — removes deal uncertainty, unlocks cross-selling and integration synergies

3.ZeroFlap hyperscaler ramp — Brennan teased expanding customer traction, next update in June could name new wins

4.3.2T product cycle — Dust is ahead of market on 448 Gbps PIC, positions CRDO as the enabling platform for next-gen port speeds

5.CPO/NPO inflection — dual roadmap (laser via Dust + microLED in-house) means CRDO wins regardless of which architecture hyperscalers choose, with scale-up driving 10x connection density

The catalyst: a definitive agreement to acquire DustPhotonics for $750M cash + stock, bringing industry-leading Silicon Photonics PIC technology in-house. This is a thesis-level event. Here’s why: The Bear Case Just Died The biggest overhang on CRDO was the copper-vs-optical debate — would optical interconnect eventually cannibalize their dominant AEC franchise? That question is now irrelevant. Credo owns both sides of the connectivity stack: AECs within racks, optical between racks and across pods. They don’t care which one wins. They win either way. What DustPhotonics Brings DustPhotonics has a differentiated SiPho PIC portfolio spanning 400G, 800G, 1.6T with a roadmap to 3.2T — already deployed at leading hyperscale AI clusters and in design for both NPO and CPO applications. This isn’t a speculative R&D bet. These are production-grade photonic integrated circuits sitting in live AI infrastructure today. The Numbers Credo now expects combined optical revenue (ZeroFlap transceivers + Optical DSPs + Silicon Photonics) to exceed $500M in fiscal 2027. For context, CRDO did ~$268M total revenue last quarter. Optical alone is on track to become a revenue pillar on par with the core AEC/IC franchise.
